title: SIMART: Decomposing Monolithic Meshes into Sim-ready Articulated Assets via MLLM
abstract: 高质量可动三维模型是具身智能与物理仿真不可或缺的要素，然而当前三维生成技术仍聚焦于静态网格，导致"仿真就绪"的交互式对象存在缺口。现有可动物体创建方法多依赖多阶段流程，各解耦模块间的误差会逐级累积。相比之下，统一的多模态大语言模型为联合静态资产理解与仿真就绪资产生成提供了单阶段解决方案。但基于稠密体素的三维标记化方法会产生冗长的三维标记序列和高昂内存开销，限制了处理复杂可动物体的扩展能力。为此，我们提出SIMART——一个统一的多模态大语言模型框架，能够同步实现部件级分解与运动学预测。通过引入稀疏三维VQ-VAE编码器，SIMART相较稠密体素标记将标记数量减少70%，从而支持高保真多部件装配。该框架在PartNet-Mobility数据集和开放域AIGC数据集上达到最先进性能，并成功应用于基于物理的机器人仿真场景。
preview_text: High-quality articulated 3D assets are indispensable for embodied AI and physical simulation, yet 3D generation still focuses on static meshes, leaving a gap in "sim-ready" interactive objects. Most recent articulated object creation methods rely on multi-stage pipelines that accumulate errors across decoupled modules. Alternatively, unified MLLMs offer a single-stage path to joint static asset understanding and sim-ready asset generation. However dense voxel-based 3D tokenization yields long 3D token sequences and high memory overhead, limiting scalability to complex articulated objects. To address this, we propose SIMART, a unified MLLM framework that jointly performs part-level decomposition and kinematic prediction. By introducing a Sparse 3D VQ-VAE, SIMART reduces token counts by 70% vs. dense voxel tokens, enabling high-fidelity multi-part assemblies. SIMART achieves state-of-the-art performance on PartNet-Mobility and in-the-wild AIGC datasets, and enables physics-based robotic simulation.
one_line_summary: SIMART是一个基于MLLM的统一框架，用于将单体网格分解为可模拟的关节化3D资产，通过稀疏3D VQ-VAE减少令牌数量，提升物理模拟性能。
